# The Mistral Large Model v2: 500 Billion Tokens, 100,000 Context Length, and More

###### Abstract

We present Mistral Large, a 7B parameter model that is the successor to the original Mistral model and the first model in the Mistral Large family. Mistral Large is trained on over 500 billion tokens, more than double the size of the training set used for the original Mistral model, and features a new sliding window attention mechanism that allows it to process inputs of up to 100,000 tokens in length. We demonstrate that Mistral Large outperforms the original Mistral model, as well as other state-of-the-art models of comparable size, across a wide range of benchmarks that measure knowledge, reasoning, and coding capabilities. We also show that the new sliding window attention mechanism is highly effective at improving the model’s long-context capabilities, enabling it to outperform other long-context models of comparable size. We make the Mistral Large model weights and code publicly available under the Apache 2.0 license.

## 1 Introduction

The recent explosion of open-source foundation models has led to a proliferation of models that are comparable in size to or even larger than the original GPT-3 model *(Brown et al., 2020)*. These models have shown impressive capabilities on a wide range of tasks, including language understanding, reasoning, and coding. However, many of these models are limited in their ability to process long inputs, with a context length of only 2048 or 4096 tokens, which is often insufficient for many practical applications.

In this paper, we present Mistral Large, a 7B parameter model that is the successor to the original Mistral model *(Jiang et al., 2023)* and the first model in the Mistral Large family. Mistral Large is trained on over 500 billion tokens, more than double the size of the training set used for the original Mistral model, and features a new sliding window attention mechanism that allows it to process inputs of up to 100,000 tokens in length. We demonstrate that Mistral Large outperforms the original Mistral model, as well as other state-of-the-art models of comparable size, across a wide range of benchmarks that measure knowledge, reasoning, and coding capabilities. We also show that the new sliding window attention mechanism is highly effective at improving the model’s long-context capabilities, enabling it to outperform other long-context models of comparable size.

Our contributions are as follows:

- We present Mistral Large, a 7B parameter model that is the successor to the original Mistral model and the first model in the Mistral Large family.
- We introduce a new sliding window attention mechanism that allows the model to process inputs of up to 100,000 tokens in length, and show that it is highly effective at improving the model’s long-context capabilities.
- We demonstrate that Mistral Large outperforms the original Mistral model, as well as other state-of-the-art models of comparable size, across a wide range of benchmarks that measure knowledge, reasoning, and coding capabilities.
- We make the Mistral Large model weights and code publicly available under the Apache 2.0 license.

The rest of the paper is organized as follows. In Section 2, we describe the model architecture and training procedure for Mistral Large. In Section 3, we present our experimental results, including comparisons to other state-of-the-art models. In Section 4, we discuss the implications of our results and the future directions for the Mistral Large family of models. Finally, in Section 5, we conclude the paper and outline the key takeaways.

## 2 Model and Training

### 2.1 Model Architecture

The Mistral Large model is based on the original Mistral model architecture, with several key modifications. First, we increase the number of layers from 24 to 32, and the hidden dimension from 4096 to 5120. Second, we introduce a new sliding window attention mechanism that allows the model to process inputs of up to 100,000 tokens in length. The sliding window attention mechanism works by dividing the input sequence into overlapping windows of a fixed size, and then applying attention within each window. The windows are then shifted one token at a time, and the process is repeated. This allows the model to capture long-range dependencies without the need for expensive global attention. The sliding window attention mechanism is described in more detail in Appendix A.

### 2.2 Training Procedure

The Mistral Large model is trained on over 500 billion tokens, which is more than double the size of the training set used for the original Mistral model. The training set is a mixture of high-quality text data from a variety of sources, including books, articles, and code. We use a similar tokenization strategy as the original Mistral model, with a vocabulary size of 32,768. The model is trained using the AdamW optimizer *(Loshchilov and Hutter, 2017)* with a learning rate of 6e-6, a batch size of 4096, and a sequence length of 16,384. We use a cosine learning rate schedule with a warmup of 2,000 steps and a total of 300,000 steps. We also use gradient clipping with a maximum gradient norm of 1.0.

### 2.3 Sliding Window Attention

The sliding window attention mechanism is a key innovation that allows the Mistral Large model to process long inputs efficiently. The basic idea is to divide the input sequence into overlapping windows of a fixed size, and then apply attention within each window. The windows are then shifted one token at a time, and the process is repeated. This allows the model to capture long-range dependencies without the need for expensive global attention.

To implement the sliding window attention mechanism, we first divide the input sequence into non-overlapping chunks of size $L$. For each chunk, we apply attention over a window of size $W$ that is centered on the current token. The attention window is then shifted one token to the right, and the process is repeated for the next chunk. This results in an attention window that is shifted by $L$ tokens for each chunk. The attention window size $W$ is a hyperparameter that can be tuned to balance the model’s ability to capture long-range dependencies with its computational efficiency.

In practice, we use an attention window size of 4096 tokens, which allows the model to capture dependencies that are up to 4096 tokens apart. This is sufficient for many practical applications, and allows the model to process inputs of up to 100,000 tokens in length efficiently.

The sliding window attention mechanism is described in more detail in Algorithm 1.

Algorithm 1 Sliding Window Attention
Input: Input sequence $x$, attention window size $W$, chunk size $L$
Output: Output sequence $y$
1. Divide the input sequence into non-overlapping chunks of size $L$: $x_{1},x_{2},...,x_{n}$
2. For each chunk $x_{i}$:
3. Apply attention over a window of size $W$ that is centered on the current token: $\hat{x}_{i}=Attention(x_{i},W)$
4. Shift the attention window one token to the right: $x_{i}=Shift(\hat{x}_{i},1)$
5. Repeat until the end of the sequence is reached
6. Concatenate the processed chunks: $y=\hat{x}_{1},\hat{x}_{2},...,\hat{x}_{n}$

## Introduction

Mistral V7 is a popular fighting game, and the Tekken 7 Patch 2.0 is a significant update that introduces new features, characters, and improvements. This guide will provide a comprehensive overview of the patch, including its key changes, new features, and how to download it.

## Key Features of Mistral V7 Tekken 7 Patch 2.0

### New Characters

The patch introduces several new characters, each with unique abilities and playstyles. Some of the new characters include:

- **Lars Alexandersson**: A former captain of the Tekken Force, Lars is a versatile character with a mix of long-range and close-range moves.
- **Alisa Bosconovitch**: A humanoid robot created by Heihachi Mishima, Alisa has a unique fighting style that combines speed and precision.
- **Eliza**: A vampire with the ability to manipulate blood, Eliza offers a mix of offensive and defensive moves.

### Gameplay Improvements

The patch includes several gameplay improvements aimed at balancing the roster and enhancing the overall experience:

- **Adjusted Move Properties**: Many moves have been tweaked to ensure fairness and consistency.
- **New Mechanics**: Introduces new mechanics like the "Rage Art" system, which allows characters to unleash powerful moves when their health is low.
- **Enhanced AI**: The AI has been improved to provide a more challenging experience, especially in the game's story mode.

### New Stages and Customization Options

The patch adds new stages set in various locations, including:

- **Mishima Zaibatsu Headquarters**: A futuristic stage with a high-tech aesthetic.
- **Geisei Temple**: A serene, traditional Japanese setting.

Additionally, there are new customization options for characters, including outfits and accessories that reflect the game's diverse roster.

### Online Play Enhancements

The patch improves the online experience with:

- **Reduced Lag**: Optimizations to minimize lag and improve connectivity.
- **New Ranked Modes**: Introduces new ranked modes that offer different challenges and rewards.

## How to Download Mistral V7 Tekken 7 Patch 2.0

### Step-by-Step Guide

1. **Check System Requirements**: Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements for the game and the patch.
2. **Back Up Your Data**: If you have save files or custom content, back them up to avoid data loss during the update.
3. **Download the Patch**: Visit the official website or the platform where you purchased the game (e.g., Steam, PlayStation Store, Xbox Live) and download the patch.
4. **Install the Patch**: Follow the installation instructions provided. Make sure not to interrupt the process.
5. **Verify the Installation**: After installation, launch the game to ensure the patch is properly applied.

### Troubleshooting

- **Patch Not Downloading**: Check your internet connection and ensure there are no restrictions on the download.
- **Game Crashes After Update**: Verify that all game files are intact and consider reinstalling the game if necessary.
- **Missing Characters or Content**: Ensure the patch is fully downloaded and installed. Restart your system if the issue persists.

## Conclusion

The Mistral V7 Tekken 7 Patch 2.0 is a significant update that brings new characters, gameplay improvements, and enhanced online play. By following the steps outlined in this guide, you can ensure a smooth installation and enjoy the new features the patch offers. If you encounter any issues, refer to the troubleshooting section or visit the official support forums for additional help.

For more information, visit the official [Mistral V7 website](https://mistralv7.com).

### Re: Is it a good idea to use a large language model to help with a project?

The use of large language models (LLMs) in project development can be highly beneficial, but it depends on how they are applied. Here’s a breakdown of the pros and cons to consider:

**Pros:**
1. **Idea Generation and Brainstorming**: LLMs can quickly generate ideas, suggest solutions, and provide insights on various aspects of a project. This can be useful for brainstorming, especially when you need diverse perspectives.
2. **Documentation and Code Assistance**: For software projects, LLMs can help write, review, or debug code, generate documentation, and even explain complex concepts. This can save time and improve code quality.
3. **Answering Questions**: LLMs can provide explanations on technical concepts, best practices, or troubleshooting steps, which can be helpful for learning or troubleshooting.
4. **Automating Repetitive Tasks**: For tasks like data cleaning, text processing, or generating boilerplate code, LLMs can automate some of the work, freeing up time for more complex tasks.

**Cons and Challenges:**
1. **Accuracy and Hallucinations**: LLMs can sometimes generate incorrect or nonsensical information. Always verify their outputs, especially for critical tasks.
2. **Lack of Deep Understanding**: LLMs don’t truly understand context or intent, so they may miss nuanced details or provide overly generic answers.
3. **Ethical and Privacy Concerns**: Be cautious about inputting sensitive or proprietary information, as some LLMs may store or share data (depending on the model’s architecture and settings).
4. **Over-Reliance**: Depending too much on LLMs can hinder your own problem-solving skills or creativity. Use them as a tool, not a substitute for your own thinking.

**Best Practices:**
- Use LLMs for inspiration or to explore options, but validate their suggestions with reliable sources or experts.
- For code, test and debug the output thoroughly. LLMs can write code, but they may not always be optimal or error-free.
- For creative or original work, use LLMs to refine your ideas rather than replacing your own creativity.

If your project is in an area where LLMs are strong (e.g., coding, documentation, or general knowledge), they can be a powerful assistant. However, for highly specialized, sensitive, or creative work, human judgment and expertise remain crucial.

# Mistral V7 Tekken 8: Unveiling the Future of Fighting Games

## Introduction

Welcome to the future of fighting games with Mistral V7 Tekken 8! This article delves into the latest advancements in the Tekken series, focusing on the revolutionary Mistral V7 engine that promises to redefine the fighting game experience. From cutting-edge graphics to enhanced gameplay mechanics, we explore what makes this installment a must-watch for fans and newcomers alike.

## The Evolution of Tekken

### A Legacy of Innovation

Tekken has always been at the forefront of fighting game innovation. From its debut in 1994 to the critically acclaimed Tekken 7, the series has consistently pushed boundaries. With Tekken 8, the franchise takes a bold step forward, introducing the Mistral V7 engine, a technological leap that sets new standards in visual fidelity and gameplay depth.

### Mistral V7: The Engine of the Future

The Mistral V7 engine is the heart of Tekken 8, designed to harness the power of next-generation hardware. This engine not only enhances graphics but also introduces advanced AI, dynamic environments, and unparalleled character customization. It's a testament to Bandai Namco's commitment to delivering an immersive and technically superior experience.

## Key Features of Mistral V7 Tekken 8

### 1. Unprecedented Graphics

#### Realistic Character Models

Mistral V7 brings characters to life with hyper-realistic models, detailed textures, and lifelike animations. Every move, from a simple jab to an intricate combo, is rendered with stunning precision.

#### Dynamic Lighting and Shadows

The engine's advanced lighting system creates a more immersive atmosphere. Real-time shadows and reflections enhance the sense of space, making the game world feel more alive.

### 2. Enhanced Gameplay Mechanics

#### Advanced Movement System

Tekken 8 introduces a revamped movement system that allows for smoother transitions between attacks and defenses. Players can now chain moves with greater fluidity, adding depth to the combat.

#### Rage System Overhaul

The Rage system has been reimagined to offer more strategic options. Players can now use Rage Art and Rage Drive more dynamically, creating new opportunities for counterattacks and combos.

### 3. Innovative AI

#### Adaptive Opponents

The AI in Tekken 8 learns from player behavior, adapting tactics in real-time to provide a more challenging and engaging experience. This feature, powered by Mistral V7, ensures that no two matches feel the same.

#### Customizable AI Behavior

For the first time, players can tweak AI settings to suit their preferences, from difficulty levels to specific fighting styles, making the game more accessible to both casual and competitive players.

### 4. Expanded Customization

#### Deep Character Customization

Mistral V7 allows for unparalleled character customization. Players can fine-tune not just appearances but also fighting styles and abilities, creating unique versions of their favorite fighters.

#### New Costume and Accessory Options

The game introduces a vast array of customization options, from classic costumes to new, concept-based designs, giving players more ways to express their creativity.

## The Future of Fighting Games

### Competitive Scene

Tekken 8 aims to elevate the competitive scene with its refined mechanics and advanced online features. The Mistral V7 engine ensures that online matches are not only visually stunning but also lag-free, providing a seamless experience for players worldwide.

### Story and Lore

The game's story mode promises to deliver a cinematic experience, with high-quality cutscenes and a gripping narrative that continues the saga of the Mishima family. The Mistral V7 engine enhances this with its powerful rendering capabilities, making every scene a visual spectacle.

## Conclusion

Mistral V7 Tekken 8 is poised to be a landmark title in the fighting game genre. With its groundbreaking engine, unmatched graphics, and innovative gameplay features, it sets a new benchmark for what fighting games can achieve. Whether you're a long-time fan or new to the series, Tekken 8 offers an experience that is both familiar and refreshingly new.

## FAQs

### What is the Mistral V7 engine?

The Mistral V7 engine is the advanced technology powering Tekken 8, designed to deliver superior graphics, enhanced AI, and dynamic gameplay features.

### How does Tekken 8 improve on previous entries?

Tekken 8 introduces more fluid movement, a revamped Rage system, adaptive AI, and deeper customization options, making it the most advanced entry in the series to date.

### What new features does the customization system offer?

Players can customize not just appearances but also fighting styles and abilities, with a wider range of costumes and accessories than ever before.

### Will Tekken 8 support cross-play?

Yes, Tekken 8 is designed to support cross-play, allowing players on different platforms to compete against each other seamlessly.

### When is the release date for Tekken 8?

As of now, the exact release date for Tekken 8 has not been officially announced, but it is highly anticipated for a 2024 launch.

The most important thing to remember when playing Tekken 7 is to always be on the move. Staying still is a death sentence. You should constantly be moving around, trying to create openings for your attacks. If you stay in one place, your opponent will be able to predict your moves and counter them.

Here are some tips to help you improve your Tekken 7 gameplay:

1. **Understand Frame Data**: Knowing the frame data of your moves is crucial. This helps you understand when your moves are safe or unsafe and how to punish your opponent's mistakes.
2. **Practice Your Combos**: Regular practice of your combos will help you execute them more efficiently in matches. Focus on the most effective combos for your character.
3. **Master the Sidestep**: Sidestepping is a fundamental defensive tool in Tekken. Mastering it can help you avoid your opponent's attacks and create openings for your own.
4. **Learn to Punish**: Look for opportunities to punish your opponent's mistakes. If they whiff a move, you should be ready to punish them with a fast, high-damage combo.
5. **Use Your Tools**: Every character has unique tools. Learn what yours are and how to use them effectively.
6. **Stay Patient**: Don't rush your attacks. Wait for your opponent to make a mistake and then punish them.
7. **Practice Defense**: Blocking and parrying are important skills. Learn when to block high or low and how to parry effectively.
8. **Play Against Different Opponents**: Each opponent has a unique playstyle. Practice against a variety of opponents to understand how to counter different strategies.

Remember, the key to improving is consistent practice. Keep playing, learn from your mistakes, and always look for ways to improve your gameplay. Good luck!

Tk7.6.12.1

This release is mostly a bug fix release, with some minor improvements.

Tk 7.6.12.1 requires Tcl 7.7.12.2 or higher. Tk 7.6.11.3 is the last release that still supports Tcl 7.7.12.1.

Tk 7.6.12.1 requires Tcl 7.7.12.2 or higher. Tk 7.6.11.3 is the last release that still supports Tcl 7.7.12.1.

The release is available in the Tk releases page at https://sourceforge.net/projects/tktoolkit/files/Tk/7.6.12.1/.

New features:

- Tk has a new option called -backdrop, which allows a window to be displayed as a backdrop. When a window is a backdrop, it is displayed in front of all other non-backdrop windows, but behind all top-level windows. The backdrop window is always on top of the root window. It is not possible to raise or lower a backdrop window. It is also not possible to move a backdrop window. The backdrop window does not have a title bar or a frame. The backdrop window is not included in the list of toplevel windows returned by wm stackorder. The backdrop window is not included in the list of toplevel windows returned by wm stackorder. The backdrop window is not included in the list of toplevel windows returned by wm stackorder.

# Mistral 7B V0.3: A 7B Parameter Open-Source AI Model

###### Abstract

We present Mistral 7B v0.3, an open-source large language model that uses a modified version of the Transformer architecture and is optimized for high performance with limited resources. The model is designed to be efficient, using only 7 billion parameters, and is capable of outperforming many larger models on a variety of natural language processing tasks. We also introduce the Mistral Instruct v0.1, a fine-tuned version of the Mistral 7B v0.3 model that is optimized for instruction-following tasks. This model has been fine-tuned on a dataset of over 200,000 human-generated instructions, resulting in a model that is able to follow instructions more accurately and effectively than the base model. In this paper, we will describe the architecture of the Mistral 7B v0.3 model, the training process, and the evaluation of the model’s performance on a variety of natural language processing tasks.

## 1 Introduction

In recent years, large language models (LLMs) have become an essential tool for a wide range of natural language processing (NLP) tasks. However, the increasing size of these models has led to concerns about their computational efficiency and the resources required for training and inference. To address this issue, we introduce Mistral 7B v0.3, a 7 billion parameter LLM that uses a modified version of the Transformer architecture to improve efficiency and reduce computational resources required for training and inference. The model is designed to be a high-performance, low-resource alternative to larger models, capable of outperforming them on a variety of tasks. Additionally, we introduce the Mistral Instruct v0.1, a fine-tuned version of the Mistral 7B v0.3 model that is optimized for instruction-following tasks. This model has been fine-tuned on a dataset of over 200,000 human-generated instructions, resulting in a model that is able to follow instructions more accurately and effectively than the base model. In this paper, we will describe the architecture of the Mistral 7B v0.3 model, the training process, and the evaluation of the model’s performance on a variety of NLP tasks.

## 2 Model Architecture

The Mistral 7B v0.3 model is based on the Transformer architecture, which has been shown to be highly effective for a variety of NLP tasks. However, we have made several modifications to the architecture to improve its efficiency and reduce its computational requirements.

### 2.1 Modified Transformer Architecture

The base Transformer architecture is a stack of self-attention layers and feed-forward layers. In the Mistral 7B v0.3 model, we use a modified version of the Transformer architecture that includes the following changes:

- Grouped Query Attention (GQA) *[x2]* to reduce the computational complexity of the self-attention layers.
- Sliding window attention *[x1]* to reduce the memory complexity of the self-attention layers.
- SwiGLU activation *[x3]* to improve the efficiency of the feed-forward layers.
- Rotary Positional Embedding (RoPE) *[x4]* to reduce the memory complexity of the positional embedding.

### 2.2 Model Size and Parameters

The Mistral 7B v0.3 model has a total of 7 billion parameters, which is significantly smaller than many state-of-the-art models. The model uses a hidden size of 4096, a feed-forward size of 14336, and 32 attention heads. The model is designed to be efficient, with a total of 12 layers in the encoder and 12 layers in the decoder.

## 3 Training Process

The Mistral 7B v0.3 model was trained on a dataset of over 1 trillion tokens, which includes a wide range of text from the internet, books, and other sources. The model was trained using a modified version of the AdamW optimizer *[x5]*, with a learning rate of 0.0006 and a batch size of 32. The model was trained for a total of 350,000 steps, with a checkpoint saved every 10,000 steps.

The Mistral Instruct v0.1 model was fine-tuned on a dataset of over 200,000 human-generated instructions, which were collected from a variety of sources, including the web, books, and other datasets. The model was fine-tuned using the same optimizer and learning rate as the base model, with a batch size of 32. The model was fine-tuned for a total of 10,000 steps, with a checkpoint saved every 1,000 steps.

## 4 Evaluation

The Mistral 7B v0.3 model was evaluated on a variety of NLP tasks, including language modeling, machine translation, and question answering. The model was compared to a number of state-of-the-art models, including the GPT-3 *[x6]*, T5 *[x7]*, and BERT *[x8]* models. The results show that the Mistral 7B v0.3 model is able to outperform or match the performance of these larger models on a variety of tasks, despite its smaller size.

The Mistral Instruct v0.1 model was evaluated on instruction-following tasks, including question answering, summarization, and text generation. The model was compared to the base Mistral 7B v0.3 model, as well as the GPT-3 model. The results show that the Mistral Instruct v0.1 model is able to follow instructions more accurately and effectively than the base model, and is able to outperform or match the performance of the GPT-3 model on a variety of tasks.

## 5 Conclusion

In this paper, we introduce the Mistral 7B v0.3 model, an open-source LLM that uses a modified version of the Transformer architecture to improve efficiency and reduce computational resources required for training and inference. We also introduce the Mistral Instruct v0.1 model, a fine-tuned version of the Mistral 7B v0.3 model that is optimized for instruction-following tasks. We describe the architecture of the model, the training process, and the evaluation of the model’s performance on a variety of NLP tasks. The results show that the Mistral 7B v0.3 model is able to outperform or match the performance of larger models on a variety of tasks, and that the Mistral Instruct v0.1 model is able to follow instructions more accurately and effectively than the base model.

We hope that the Mistral 7B v0.3 and Mistral Instruct v0.1 models will be useful tools for the research community, and that they will help to advance the field of NLP. We also hope that the model’s efficiency and performance will make it a valuable tool for deployment in real-world applications.

### Summary:
Each version builds on the last, with v0.4 being the most advanced, addressing many of the shortcomings of earlier versions. The focus has been on making the model more practical for real-world tasks like coding, math, and extended conversations while maintaining natural language fluency.

# Mistral V7: A 50B Parameter Model for the Tekken Universe

###### Abstract

Mistral AI, a leading provider of open-weight models, is releasing the latest in its lineup of cutting-edge models: Mistral V7, a 50B parameter model that is optimized for the Tekken universe. Trained on a massive corpus of Tekken data, including game lore, character bios, and move lists, Mistral V7 is designed to provide unparalleled insights into the world of Tekken. It can help players learn new strategies, generate custom move lists, and even create new character bios. Whether you’re a seasoned player or a newcomer to the series, Mistral V7 is your go-to resource for all things Tekken. So, power up your game and dive into the world of Tekken like never before with Mistral V7.

## 1 Introduction

In the ever-evolving landscape of the Tekken universe, where the clash of fists and the dance of moves define the essence of competition, a new era of understanding and mastery emerges with Mistral V7. This 50B parameter model, meticulously trained on a vast corpus of Tekken-related data, stands as a beacon for players seeking to delve deeper into the intricate world of the King of Iron Fist Tournament. From the lore that binds the characters to the moves that define their combat styles, Mistral V7 is poised to be the ultimate companion for both seasoned players and newcomers alike.

The Tekken universe, with its rich history and diverse roster of characters, has captivated millions of players worldwide. Each character brings a unique set of skills, a backstory that intertwines with the others, and a fighting style that can be mastered only through practice and insight. Mistral V7 aims to bridge the gap between the data and the player, offering a platform where the nuances of the game can be explored, analyzed, and even expanded upon.

In this paper, we explore the capabilities of Mistral V7, from its training on Tekken-specific data to its applications in strategy generation, custom move list creation, and the generation of new character bios. We delve into how this model can assist players in understanding the intricacies of the game, from the subtle differences between characters to the optimal strategies for different matchups. We also discuss the potential for Mistral V7 to enhance the community’s creative endeavors, such as fan fiction and custom content, by providing a tool that can generate original and contextually appropriate content.

As we embark on this journey, we aim to demonstrate that Mistral V7 is not just a model but a gateway to a deeper, more nuanced appreciation of the Tekken universe. ### How to install Debian 12 on a laptop with an NVIDIA graphics card?

I have a laptop with an NVIDIA graphics card (GeForce RTX 3050) and I want to install Debian 12 on it. What do I need to do to make sure that the graphics card is properly supported? Do I need to install any specific drivers? If so, which ones? Also, are there any special considerations for using NVIDIA cards with Debian?

### Re: How to install Debian 12 on a laptop with an NVIDIA graphics card?

For a smooth installation of Debian 12 (Bookworm) on a laptop with an NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 graphics card, you'll need to install the appropriate proprietary drivers. Here's a step-by-step guide:

#### 1. **Choose the Right Installation Media**
   - Download the Debian 12 non-free ISO from the official website. The "non-free" ISO includes non-free firmware and drivers, which are necessary for NVIDIA cards.
   - Alternatively, you can use the standard ISO and install the drivers later, but the non-free ISO will save you some steps.

#### 2. **Install Debian with Default Drivers**
   - Boot from the installation media and proceed with the installation. During the installation, Debian will use the open-source `nouveau` driver by default, which works but may lack full functionality (like hardware acceleration).
   - Complete the installation as usual.

#### 3. **Install Proprietary NVIDIA Drivers**
   After installation, open a terminal and run the following commands to add the non-free repository and install the drivers:

   ```bash
   sudo apt update
   sudo apt install -y firmware-misc-nonfree nvidia-driver firmware-nvidia-graphics
   ```

   - For the RTX 3050, the `nvidia-driver` package (usually the 535 or 545 series) should work. Debian 12 should automatically select the latest stable driver.
   - Reboot after installation.

#### 4. **Verify the Driver is Working**
   After reboot, run the following to check the active driver:

   ```bash
   nvidia-smi
   ```

   This should display details about your GPU, confirming the driver is active.

#### 5. **Optional: Install CUDA (if needed)**
   If you plan to use CUDA for development, you can install it from NVIDIA's website or via Debian's repositories:

   ```bash
   sudo apt install -y nvidia-cuda-toolkit
   ```

#### 6. **Handling Secure Boot (if applicable)**
   - If your system has Secure Boot enabled, you may need to sign the NVIDIA modules. You can do this during driver installation or disable Secure Boot in the BIOS.

#### 7. **Troubleshooting**
   - If you encounter issues, try installing a specific driver version (e.g., `nvidia-driver-535`).
   - If the screen is blank after reboot, switch to a TTY (Ctrl+Alt+F3), log in, and run:
     ```bash
     sudo apt install -y xserver-xorg-video-nouveau
     sudo systemctl set-default multi-user.target
     ```
     to temporarily revert to the open-source driver and fix the issue.

#### 8. **Hybrid Graphics (if applicable)**
   - If your laptop has an integrated Intel/AMD GPU alongside the NVIDIA card, you may need to use `prime-select` to switch between them:
     ```bash
     sudo apt install -y nvidia-prime
     sudo prime-select nvidia
     ```

This should give you full NVIDIA driver support on Debian 12.

# Tekken 7: A Comprehensive Game Guide

Welcome to the ultimate guide to Tekken 7! Whether you're a newcomer or an experienced fighter, this guide will provide you with all the essential information to master the game.

## Getting Started

### Basic Controls

- **Movement**: Analog stick to move, d-pad for precise movements.
- **Attacks**: Face buttons for punches and kicks. Each button corresponds to a different strength and type of attack.
  - Square (X) - High punch
  - Triangle (Y) - High kick
  - Circle (B) - Low punch
  - X (A) - Low kick
- **Blocking**: Hold back to block high attacks, hold down to block low attacks.

### Game Modes

- **Arcade Mode**: Fight through a series of opponents to face the final boss.
- **Versus Mode**: Play against a friend or the CPU.
- **Treasure Battle**: Collect treasures and fight opponents to unlock new items and characters.
- **Story Mode**: Experience the story of the Tekken 7 characters.

## Character Selection

Tekken 7 features a diverse roster of characters, each with unique moves and playstyles. Here are some notable characters:

- **Paul Phoenix**: Fast and aggressive, with strong punches and kicks.
- **Kazuya Mishima**: Balanced character with a mix of powerful moves and quick attacks.
- **Heihachi Mishima**: Strong and aggressive, with a focus on powerful strikes.
- **Jin Kazama**: Versatile fighter with a mix of long-range and close-range attacks.
- **Lars Alexandersson**: Well-rounded character with a mix of defensive and offensive moves.

## Gameplay Mechanics

### Combos

- **Basic Combos**: Simple sequences of attacks that can be easily executed.
  - Example: Square, Square, Triangle for a quick combo.
- **Advanced Combos**: More complex sequences that require precise timing and inputs.
  - Example: Square, Square, Triangle, Circle, Circle for a more powerful combo.

### Special Moves

Each character has unique special moves that can be executed with specific button combinations. Practice these moves to master your character's playstyle.

- **Kazuya's Devil Form**: Press R1 + any direction to transform into Devil Kazuya, gaining powerful new moves.
- **Jin's Electric Moves**: Press L1 + any direction to unleash electric attacks.

### Rage System

- **Rage Arts**: When your health is low, you can activate a powerful move that deals massive damage.
- **Rage Drive**: When your health is critical, you can activate this move for an even more powerful attack.

## Advanced Techniques

### Sidestepping

- Move sideways to avoid attacks and create openings for counterattacks.

### Parrying

- Press a specific button just before an attack lands to block it and create an opening for a counterattack.

### Juggling

- Keep your opponent in the air with a sequence of attacks to deal continuous damage.

## Tips for Beginners

1. **Practice the Basics**: Focus on mastering basic controls and combos before moving on to advanced techniques.
2. **Learn Your Character**: Spend time learning your character's moves and strengths.
3. **Defensive Play**: Don't just focus on attacking; learn to block and counter effectively.
4. **Watch and Learn**: Observe how other players play and learn from their strategies.

## Multiplayer

- **Online Play**: Connect to the internet to play against other players worldwide.
- **Ranked Matches**: Climb the leaderboards by winning ranked matches.
- **Custom Matches**: Create your own matches with specific rules and settings.

## Unlocking Content

- **Characters**: Complete the story mode or win a certain number of matches to unlock new characters.
- **Costumes**: Collect treasures in Treasure Battle mode to unlock new costumes for your characters.

## Conclusion

Tekken 7 is a game of skill, strategy, and practice. Whether you're playing for fun or aiming to be the best, this guide provides the foundation you need to succeed. Keep practicing, and soon you'll be a Tekken 7 master!

# Mistral V7: A 7B Parameter Open-Source LLM with 1.2T Tokens of Data

###### Abstract

We introduce Mistral V7, a 7B parameter language model trained on 1.2T tokens of data, a combination of the original OpenWebMath dataset and the OpenWebText2 dataset. Mistral V7 achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source models on the MMLU benchmark, surpassing GPT-4 with 89.2% accuracy on the 5-shot setting. Additionally, we achieve competitive performance on the 0-shot setting of the MT-Bench benchmark. Mistral V7 is the first model to combine the benefits of the OpenWebMath and OpenWebText2 datasets, resulting in a model that excels at both mathematics and general language tasks. The model and code are available at https://github.com/open-web-math/OpenWebMath.

## 1 Introduction

In this work, we introduce Mistral V7, a 7B parameter language model that has been trained on a 1.2T tokens of data, combining the OpenWebMath *[1]* and OpenWebText2 *[2]* datasets. The OpenWebMath dataset is an extensive collection of mathematical web content, while the OpenWebText2 dataset is a collection of English-language text from a wide range of sources. By combining these datasets, we aim to create a model that can excel at both mathematics and general language tasks.

The Mistral V7 model is based on the architecture of the original Mistral model *[3]*, which has a feed-forward network with 16,384 dimensions and a head size of 64. We used the same architecture for our model, but we increased the number of layers to 32 to improve performance. We also increased the context size to 4K tokens to allow for more complex and longer inputs.

We trained the Mistral V7 model using a combination of the OpenWebMath and OpenWebText2 datasets. The OpenWebMath dataset contains a variety of mathematical content, including equations, proofs, and mathematical discussions, while the OpenWebText2 dataset contains a wide range of English-language text, including news articles, blog posts, and social media posts. By combining these two datasets, we aim to create a model that can understand and generate both mathematical and general language content.

To evaluate the performance of the Mistral V7 model, we tested it on the MMLU benchmark, which measures the model’s ability to solve mathematical problems. The model achieved a 5-shot accuracy of 89.2% on this benchmark, which is significantly higher than the performance of other open-source models. We also tested the model on the MT-Bench benchmark, which measures the model’s ability to generate natural language text. The model performed competitively on this benchmark as well.

The Mistral V7 model is the first model to combine the benefits of the OpenWebMath and OpenWebText2 datasets, resulting in a model that excels at both mathematics and general language tasks. We believe that this model will be useful for a variety of applications, including mathematical problem-solving, natural language generation, and language translation. We plan to continue improving the model and to make it available to the research community.

## 2 Related Work

The OpenWebMath dataset *[1]* is a large-scale dataset of mathematical content that has been used to train a variety of language models for mathematical problem-solving. The OpenWebMath dataset contains a variety of mathematical content, including equations, proofs, and mathematical discussions, and has been used to train models such as the OpenWebMath model *[1]*, the OpenWebMath-7B model *[4]*, and the OpenWebMath-70B model *[5]*.

The OpenWebText2 dataset *[2]* is a large-scale dataset of English-language text that has been used to train a variety of language models for natural language generation. The OpenWebText2 dataset contains a wide range of English-language text, including news articles, blog posts, and social media posts, and has been used to train models such as the OpenWebText2 model *[2]*, the OpenWebText2-7B model *[6]*, and the OpenWebText2-70B model *[7]*.

The Mistral model *[3]* is a 7B parameter language model that has been trained on a large-scale dataset of English-language text. The Mistral model has been used as a base model for a variety of applications, including natural language generation, language translation, and text classification.

## 3 Model

The Mistral V7 model is a 7B parameter language model that is based on the architecture of the original Mistral model *[3]*. The architecture of the Mistral V7 model is shown in Figure 1. The model has 32 layers of the transformer architecture, with a feed-forward network of 16,384 dimensions and a head size of 64. The model has a context size of 4K tokens, which allows for longer and more complex inputs.

The input to the model is a sequence of tokens, which are represented as vectors in the model’s embedding space. The tokens are first passed through an embedding layer, which maps the tokens to vectors. The vectors are then passed through a series of transformer layers, which apply self-attention and feed-forward networks to the vectors. The output of the transformer layers is a set of vectors, which are then passed through a linear layer to produce the final output.

The Mistral V7 model is trained using a combination of the OpenWebMath and OpenWebText2 datasets. The OpenWebMath dataset is used to train the model on mathematical content, while the OpenWebText2 dataset is used to train the model on general language content. The two datasets are combined during training, with the OpenWebMath dataset being used for 50% of the training steps and the OpenWebText2 dataset being used for the remaining 50% of the training steps.

The loss function used to train the model is the cross-entropy loss, which is a standard loss function for language modeling. The model is trained using the AdamW optimizer *[8]*, with a learning rate of 3e-4 and a weight decay of 0.1. The model is trained for a total of 120K steps, which is sufficient to achieve convergence.

Figure 1: Architecture of the Mistral V7 model. The model has 32 layers of the transformer architecture, with a feed-forward network of 16,384 dimensions and a head size of 64. The context size of the model is 4K tokens.

## 4 Experiments

To evaluate the performance of the Mistral V7 model, we tested it on the MMLU and MT-Bench benchmarks. The MMLU benchmark measures the model’s ability to solve mathematical problems, while the MT-Bench benchmark measures the model’s ability to generate natural language text.

### 4.1 MMLU Benchmark

The MMLU benchmark is a set of 25,000 multiple-choice questions that cover a wide range of mathematical topics, including algebra, geometry, calculus, and probability. The questions are designed to test the model’s ability to understand and apply mathematical concepts. The 5-shot accuracy of the Mistral V7 model on the MMLU benchmark is shown in Table 1. The model achieves a 5-shot accuracy of 89.2%, which is significantly higher than the performance of other open-source models.

| Model | 5-shot Accuracy |
| --- | --- |
| Mistral V7 | 89.2% |
| GPT-4 | 88.5% |
| Claude-2 | 84.0% |
| PaLM-2 | 77.1% |
| OpenWebMath-7B | 84.0% |
| OpenWebText2-7B | 65.1% |

Table 1: 5-shot accuracy of the Mistral V7 model and other models on the MMLU benchmark. The Mistral V7 model achieves a 5-shot accuracy of 89.2%, which is higher than the performance of other open-source models.

### 4.2 MT-Bench Benchmark

The MT-Bench benchmark is a set of 200 multiple-choice questions that cover a wide range of natural language generation topics, including question answering, translation, and summarization. The questions are designed to test the model’s ability to generate coherent and natural-sounding text. The 0-shot accuracy of the Mistral V7 model on the MT-Bench benchmark is shown in Table 2. The model achieves a 0-shot accuracy of 8.4, which is competitive with the performance of other open-source models.

| Model | 0-shot Accuracy |
| --- | --- |
| GPT-4 | 8.6 |
| Claude-2 | 8.2 |
| Mistral V7 | 8.4 |
| OpenWebMath-7B | 7.2 |
| OpenWebText2-7B | 7.1 |

Table 2: 0-shot accuracy of the Mistral V7 model and other models on the MT-Bench benchmark. The Mistral V7 model achieves a 0-shot accuracy of 8.4, which is competitive with the performance of other open-source models.

## 5 Conclusion

In this work, we introduced the Mistral V7 model, a 7B parameter language model that is trained on a combination of the OpenWebMath and OpenWebText2 datasets. The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MMLU benchmark and competitive performance on the MT-Bench benchmark. The Mistral V7 model is the first model to combine the benefits of the OpenWebMath and OpenWebText2 datasets, resulting in a model that excels at both mathematics and general language tasks.

We believe that the Mistral V7 model will be a useful tool for a variety of applications, including mathematical problem-solving, natural language generation, and language translation. We plan to continue improving the model and to make it available to the research community.

## 6 Appendix

### 6.1 Training Details

The Mistral V7 model is trained using the OpenWebMath and OpenWebText2 datasets. The OpenWebMath dataset is used for 50% of the training steps and the OpenWebText2 dataset is used for the remaining 50% of the training steps. The model is trained using the AdamW optimizer *[8]* with a learning rate of 3e-4 and a weight decay of 0.1. The model is trained for a total of 120K steps, which is sufficient to achieve convergence.

The OpenWebMath dataset contains a total of 1.2T tokens of data, which is used for 50% of the training steps. The OpenWebText2 dataset also contains a total of 1.2T tokens of data, which is used for the remaining 50% of the training steps. The total number of tokens used for training is 2.4T, which is a large-scale dataset that is sufficient to train a high-performance language model.

### 6.2 Evaluation Details

The MMLU benchmark is evaluated using the 5-shot setting, where the model is given 5 examples of the type of question it will be asked to answer. The model is then asked to answer a question of the same type, and its answer is compared to the correct answer to determine whether it is correct or not. The accuracy of the model is the percentage of questions it answers correctly.

The MT-Bench benchmark is evaluated using the 0-shot setting, where the model is not given any examples of the type of question it will be asked to answer. The model is asked to answer a question of the type, and its answer is compared to the correct answer to determine whether it is correct or not. The accuracy of the model is the percentage of questions it answers correctly.

### 6.3 Code and Data Availability

The code and data for the Mistral V7 model will be made available at https://github.com/open-web-math/OpenWebMath. The repository will contain the code for training and evaluating the model, as well as the pretrained model weights. We hope that the research community will find this model useful for a variety of applications.

## Homework Statement

The equation of motion of a system is given by
$$\ddot{x} + \omega^2 x = 0 $$
where x is a function of t, and ω is a constant. Show that the general solution of this equation is
$$x = A \sin(\omega t) + B \cos(\omega t)$$
where A and B are constants.

## Homework Equations

The differential equation given is
$$\ddot{x} + \omega^2 x = 0 $$
The solution is
$$x = A \sin(\omega t) + B \cos(\omega t)$$

## The Attempt at a Solution

This is a second order differential equation with constant coefficients. I know that the characteristic equation is
$$r^2 + \omega^2 = 0 $$
The roots of which are
$$r = \pm \omega i$$
Therefore, the general solution is given by
$$x = C_1 e^{\omega i t} + C_2 e^{-\omega i t}$$
where C_1 and C_2 are constants. However, the question states the solution as
$$x = A \sin(\omega t) + B \cos(\omega t)$$
Can I show that these two forms of the solution are equivalent?

## The Attempt at a Solution

The general solution of the equation is
$$x = C_1 e^{\omega i t} + C_2 e^{-\omega i t}$$
$$= C_1 (\cos(\omega t) + i \sin(\omega t)) + C_2 (\cos(\omega t) - i \sin(\omega t))$$
$$= (C_1 + C_2) \cos(\omega t) + i (C_1 - C_2) \sin(\omega t)$$
Now, let
$$A = i (C_1 - C_2)$$
$$B = C_1 + C_2$$
Therefore,
$$x = A \sin(\omega t) + B \cos(\omega t)$$
This is the form that the question states.

But, can I conclude that the two forms are equivalent? This is because A and B are arbitrary constants. Since C_1 and C_2 are arbitrary constants, A and B are also arbitrary constants. Is this correct?

Thank you.

is a text generation model that has been fine-tuned for the Tekken fighting game series. It is designed to generate responses to user prompts in the style and tone of the Tekken series, including its characters, lore, and gameplay mechanics. The model is intended to be used for creative writing, role-playing, and other applications related to the Tekken franchise.

## Model Details

- **Model type:** Mistral-v7-tekken is a fine-tuned model based on the Mistral-v7 architecture, which is a transformer-based text generation model.
- **Language:** English
- **License:** MIT
- **Fine-tuning method:** The model was fine-tuned using a dataset of Tekken-related text, including dialogue, lore, and gameplay descriptions.

## Usage

Mistral-v7-tekken can be used to generate a wide range of content related to the Tekken series, such as:

- Character biographies and backstories
- Fight descriptions and strategies
- In-universe dialogue and interactions
- Storyline and scenario ideas

Here's an example of how to use the model:

```python
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer

model_name = "mistral-v7-tekken"
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name)
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_name)

prompt = "Heihachi Mishima: 'I have been waiting for this day, Kazuya. You are a disgrace to the Mishima name. But now, you will die by my hand!'"
inputs = tokenizer(prompt, return_tensors="pt")
outputs = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=50)
print(tokenizer.decode(outputs[0], skip_special_tokens=True))
```

## Limitations and Biases

- The model may generate text that is not consistent with the official Tekken lore or canon.
- The model's responses may be influenced by the biases present in the training data.
- The model may not always generate coherent or grammatically correct text.

The model was fine-tuned on a dataset of Tekken-related text, which included dialogue from the games, character bios, and lore descriptions. The fine-tuning process used the following parameters:

- Learning rate: 5e-5
- Batch size: 8

# mistral-v7-tekken: An Efficient and Versatile Multi-Modal Model for Video Understanding

###### Abstract

We introduce mistral-v7-tekken, a compact, efficient, and versatile multi-modal model that excels in video understanding tasks. The model builds upon a strong foundation of vision and language processing, employing an encoder-decoder architecture to support various applications. mistral-v7-tekken utilizes a streamlined design that integrates multiple components into a single, unified model, significantly reducing the computational and memory overhead compared to existing approaches. The key innovations include an efficient attention mechanism that optimizes the model’s performance across different scales, a unified approach to both video and language tasks, and a novel pre-training strategy that leverages large-scale datasets. The model demonstrates superior performance in benchmarks such as video question answering, video captioning, and video-text retrieval, outperforming many existing models while maintaining high efficiency. mistral-v7-tekken’s versatility and efficiency make it suitable for a wide range of applications, from real-time video analysis to interactive systems.

## 1 Introduction

Video understanding is a critical area of research in computer vision and artificial intelligence, with applications ranging from content moderation to autonomous systems. Traditional approaches often rely on separate models for different tasks, leading to inefficiencies in both training and inference. Additionally, many existing models are designed for specific tasks, lacking the versatility to handle a wide range of video-related tasks effectively.

To address these challenges, we introduce mistral-v7-tekken, an efficient and versatile multi-modal model that integrates vision and language processing capabilities into a unified architecture. The model is designed to handle various video understanding tasks, including video question answering, video captioning, and video-text retrieval, while maintaining high efficiency and performance. The key innovations include an efficient attention mechanism that adapts to different scales, a unified approach to both video and language tasks, and a novel pre-training strategy that leverages large-scale datasets.

Figure 1: Model architecture.

The architecture of mistral-v7-tekken is built around an encoder-decoder framework, where the encoder processes input data to extract relevant features, and the decoder generates outputs based on these features. The model uses a streamlined design that integrates multiple components into a single, unified model, reducing the computational and memory overhead. By using a shared set of parameters across different tasks, the model achieves significant efficiency gains without compromising performance.

The efficient attention mechanism in mistral-v7-tekken is designed to adapt to different scales, allowing the model to focus on relevant features at varying levels of granularity. This mechanism is crucial for tasks that require both coarse and fine-grained analysis, such as video captioning and question answering. Additionally, the model employs a unified approach to both video and language tasks, enabling it to seamlessly integrate visual and textual information for more accurate and context-aware outputs.

The pre-training strategy for mistral-v7-tekken involves leveraging large-scale datasets that include diverse video and text data. This strategy ensures that the model can generalize well across different tasks and domains. The model is trained on a variety of tasks, including image-text matching, video captioning, and video question answering, to build a robust understanding of both visual and textual information.

In this paper, we present the architecture and design of mistral-v7-tekken, discuss its key innovations, and evaluate its performance on various benchmarks. We demonstrate that the model achieves state-of-the-art results in video understanding tasks while maintaining high efficiency and scalability. The versatility and efficiency of mistral-v7-tekken make it suitable for a wide range of applications, from real-time video analysis to interactive systems.

## 2 Related Work

Video understanding has been a focus of research for many years, with significant advancements in recent years due to the rise of deep learning. Traditional approaches often rely on separate models for different tasks, such as action recognition, video captioning, and video question answering. However, these approaches often lack the versatility to handle multiple tasks effectively and efficiently.

Recent advancements in multi-modal models have shown promise in integrating vision and language tasks. Models like CLIP *[1]* and ALIGN *[2]* have demonstrated strong performance in image-text matching, while models like VideoBERT *[3]* and VideoBERT 2.0 *[4]* have extended this to video data. However, many of these models are designed for specific tasks and lack the versatility to handle a wide range of video understanding tasks.

Efficient attention mechanisms have been a key area of research in improving model performance. Models like Transformer *[5]* and its variants have been widely used for their ability to capture long-range dependencies. However, their high computational cost has led to the development of more efficient mechanisms, such as those used in models like Linformer *[6]*, which reduce the computational complexity of the attention mechanism.

Unified models that integrate multiple tasks into a single architecture have also gained traction. Models like UniT *[7]* and UniVi *[8]* have shown promise in handling both vision and language tasks in a unified framework. However, these models often lack the efficiency and scalability required for real-world applications.

In this paper, we introduce mistral-v7-tekken, a model that addresses these challenges by integrating vision and language processing capabilities into a unified architecture. The model employs an efficient attention mechanism, a unified approach to both video and language tasks, and a novel pre-training strategy that leverages large-scale datasets. We demonstrate that the model achieves state-of-the-art performance in various video understanding tasks while maintaining high efficiency and scalability.

## 3 Model

### 3.1 Architecture

The architecture of mistral-v7-tekken is designed to be both efficient and versatile, capable of handling various video understanding tasks. The model follows an encoder-decoder framework, where the encoder processes input data to extract features, and the decoder generates outputs based on these features. The key components of the architecture are as follows:

- Vision Encoder: The vision encoder processes video frames to extract visual features. It consists of a series of convolutional and attention layers that capture both spatial and temporal information. The encoder is designed to be efficient, using techniques such as depthwise separable convolutions to reduce computational cost.
- Language Encoder: The language encoder processes textual data to extract semantic features. It is based on the Transformer architecture, with self-attention and feed-forward layers that capture the context and relationships within the text. The language encoder is designed to be versatile, capable of handling both short and long texts.
- Multi-Modal Fusion: The multi-modal fusion module integrates features from the vision and language encoders. This module uses a cross-attention mechanism to align visual and textual features, ensuring that the model can effectively integrate both types of information. The fusion module is designed to be efficient, using a streamlined architecture that minimizes computational overhead.
- Decoder: The decoder generates outputs based on the integrated features from the fusion module. It is based on the Transformer architecture, with self-attention and feed-forward layers that generate outputs for different tasks. The decoder is designed to be versatile, capable of handling tasks such as video question answering, video captioning, and video-text retrieval. The model uses a shared set of parameters for the decoder to reduce computational and memory overhead.

### 3.2 Efficient Attention Mechanism

The efficient attention mechanism is a key component of mistral-v7-tekken, designed to optimize the model’s performance across different scales. The mechanism is based on the Transformer architecture, but with several optimizations to reduce computational complexity:

- Low-Rank Factorization: The attention mechanism uses low-rank factorization to approximate the attention matrix. This reduces the computational complexity of the attention operation, allowing the model to handle large-scale data efficiently.
- Local Attention: The mechanism incorporates local attention, which focuses on a limited number of neighboring tokens. This reduces the computational cost of the attention operation, especially for long sequences.
- Adaptive Scaling: The mechanism uses adaptive scaling to adjust the attention weights based on the input data. This allows the model to focus on relevant features at different scales, improving its performance on tasks that require both coarse and fine-grained analysis.

### 3.3 Unified Approach to Video and Language Tasks

The unified approach to video and language tasks is another key innovation in mistral-v7-tekken. The model integrates both types of information into a single architecture, enabling it to generate context-aware outputs that combine visual and textual features. The unified approach is implemented as follows:

- Joint Pre-Training: The model is pre-trained on a joint dataset that includes both video and text data. This ensures that the model can effectively integrate both types of information during training. The pre-training strategy includes tasks such as image-text matching, video captioning, and video question answering, which help the model build a robust understanding of both visual and textual information.
- Cross-Attention Fusion: The multi-modal fusion module uses cross-attention to align visual and textual features. This allows the model to generate outputs that take into account both types of information, improving its performance on tasks that require a combination of visual and textual understanding.

### 3.4 Pre-Training Strategy

The pre-training strategy for mistral-v7-tekken is designed to leverage large-scale datasets that include diverse video and text data. The strategy involves the following steps:

- Data Collection: The model is trained on a large-scale dataset that includes videos and texts from various domains. The dataset is carefully curated to ensure diversity and balance, with a focus on tasks such as image-text matching, video captioning, and video question answering.
- Task-Specific Pre-Training: The model is pre-trained on specific tasks to build a robust understanding of both visual and textual information. For example, the model is trained on image-text matching to learn to align visual and textual features, on video captioning to learn to generate descriptive text for videos, and on video question answering to learn to answer questions about videos.
- Joint Fine-Tuning: After pre-training, the model is fine-tuned on a joint dataset that includes multiple tasks. This ensures that the model can generalize well across different tasks and domains.

## 4 Experiments

### 4.1 Datasets

We evaluate mistral-v7-tekken on several benchmarks for video understanding. The datasets used are as follows:

- MSRVTT-QA: A dataset for video question answering, containing 10,000 videos and 140,000 question-answer pairs.
- MSVD-QA: A dataset for video question answering, containing 1,970 videos and 50,505 question-answer pairs.
- MSR-VTT: A dataset for video captioning, containing 10,000 videos and 200,000 captions.
- YouTube-8M: A dataset for video-text retrieval, containing 7 million videos and 1 billion labels.

### 4.2 Evaluation Metrics

The evaluation metrics used are as follows:

- Video Question Answering: Accuracy is used to evaluate the model’s performance on the MSRVTT-QA and MSVD-QA datasets.
- Video Captioning: BLEU, METEOR, and ROUGE are used to evaluate the model’s performance on the MSR-VTT dataset.
- Video-Text Retrieval: Recall@K and Precision@K are used to evaluate the model’s performance on the YouTube-8M dataset.

### 4.3 Results

The results for mistral-v7-tekken are as follows:

- Video Question Answering: The model achieves an accuracy of 75.2% on MSRVTT-QA and 72.1% on MSVD-QA, outperforming many existing models.
- Video Captioning: The model achieves a BLEU score of 18.3, a METEOR score of 25.4, and a ROUGE score of 32.1 on the MSR-VTT dataset, demonstrating strong performance in generating descriptive text for videos.
- Video-Text Retrieval: The model achieves a Recall@1000 score of 92.3% and a Precision@1000 score of 89.2% on the YouTube-8M dataset, indicating strong performance in retrieving relevant videos based on textual queries.

These results demonstrate that mistral-v7-tekken achieves state-of-the-art performance in various video understanding tasks while maintaining high efficiency and scalability.

### 4.4 Ablation Study

To evaluate the contributions of different components of the model, we conduct an ablation study. The results are as follows:

- Vision Encoder: Removing the vision encoder reduces the model’s accuracy on MSRVTT-QA to 72.3% and on MSVD-QA to 69.5%, indicating the importance of the vision encoder in capturing visual features.
- Language Encoder: Removing the language encoder reduces the model’s BLEU score on MSR-VTT to 16.1 and its Recall@1000 score on YouTube-8M to 89.2%, indicating the importance of the language encoder in capturing textual features.
- Multi-Modal Fusion: Removing the multi-modal fusion module reduces the model’s accuracy on MSRVTT-QA to 70.2% and its Recall@1000 score on YouTube-8M to 87.5%, indicating the importance of the fusion module in integrating visual and textual features.
- Efficient Attention: Using a standard attention mechanism reduces the model’s accuracy on MSRVTT-QA to 73.4% and its Recall@1000 score on YouTube-8M to 90.1%, indicating the importance of the efficient attention mechanism in optimizing the model’s performance.

These results demonstrate the contributions of different components of the model and their importance in achieving high performance.

## 5 Conclusion

In this paper, we introduce mistral-v7-tekken, an efficient and versatile multi-modal model for video understanding. The model integrates vision and language processing capabilities into a unified architecture, employing an efficient attention mechanism, a unified approach to both video and language tasks, and a novel pre-training strategy that leverages large-scale datasets. We evaluate the model on several benchmarks for video understanding and demonstrate that it achieves state-of-the-art performance while maintaining high efficiency and scalability.

The versatility and efficiency of mistral-v7-tekken make it suitable for a wide range of applications, from real-time video analysis to interactive systems. Future work could explore further optimizations and extensions to the model, such as incorporating more advanced attention mechanisms or exploring new applications in video understanding.

# Mistral Large 2 V1: Technical Report

###### Abstract

We present Mistral Large 2 (ML2), a family of large language models with 12.9 billion and 7 billion parameters. ML2 is built with the same architecture as its predecessor, Mistral 7B, and is trained on a vast amount of data, including a substantial amount of code. The model demonstrates strong performance on code generation, multi-lingual capabilities, and reasoning benchmarks. We also release Mistral 7B v0.3, a version of our 7B model trained on a refined data mix with improved performance on common benchmarks.

## 1 Introduction

Large language models (LLMs) have seen rapid advancements in the last three years, leading to significant improvements in performance across a wide range of tasks. These advances can be attributed to several factors, including larger and more diverse datasets, more sophisticated model architectures, and improved training methodologies.

In this paper, we introduce Mistral Large 2 (ML2), a family of large language models with 12.9 billion and 7 billion parameters. ML2 builds upon the architecture of its predecessor, Mistral 7B, while leveraging a substantial amount of new data, including a significant portion of code, to deliver improved performance on benchmarks and practical applications.

ML2-12.9B demonstrates strong capabilities across various tasks, excelling particularly in code generation, multi-lingual tasks, and reasoning benchmarks. We also release Mistral 7B v0.3, an optimized version of our 7B model trained on a refined data mix, showing enhanced performance on common benchmarks.

## 2 Model

Mistral Large 2 (ML2) is a family of large language models with 12.9 billion (ML2-12.9B) and 7 billion (ML2-7B) parameters. It builds on the architecture of its predecessor, Mistral 7B *(Mistral AI, 2023)*, and is trained on a large corpus of text and code data. The model is optimized for both inference and training speed, with particular emphasis on efficient memory access for the key-value cache. ML2-12.9B is trained for 4.5 trillion tokens, while ML2-7B is trained for 3 trillion tokens.

### 2.1 Architecture

Mistral Large 2 follows the same architecture as Mistral 7B *(Mistral AI, 2023)*, with 44 layers, 4096-dimensional hidden states, and 128-dimensional feed-forward layers. The model uses sliding window attention with a context length of 128k, and its vocabulary is the same as Mistral 7B, with 32k tokens. This architecture has been shown to provide a good balance between model size, training cost, and performance. We did not explore model architecture improvements in this release, as we believe that the current architecture is a solid foundation for future work.

### 2.2 Data

Mistral Large 2 is trained on a large corpus of text and code data, totaling over 10 trillion tokens. We have carefully selected and curated the data to ensure high quality and diversity. The data includes a wide range of languages, domains, and tasks, which helps the model to understand and generate text in various contexts. The code data is particularly important for the model’s performance on code generation tasks, as it exposes the model to a wide range of programming languages and code snippets. We have also included a significant amount of data in non-English languages to improve the model’s multi-lingual capabilities.

## 3 Evaluation

Mistral Large 2 demonstrates strong performance on a variety of benchmarks. In this section, we present some of the results.

### 3.1 Code Generation

We evaluated Mistral Large 2 on several code generation benchmarks. The model shows strong performance on these tasks, particularly on the HumanEval *(Chen et al., 2021)* and MBPP *(Austin et al., 2021)* benchmarks. We also evaluated the model on the CodeContests *(Bajpai et al., 2023)* benchmark, which is a more challenging and realistic benchmark for code generation. The model performs well on this benchmark as well, demonstrating its ability to generate code that is not only syntactically correct but also semantically meaningful and useful.

### 3.2 Multi-lingual Capabilities

Mistral Large 2 is trained on a significant amount of data in non-English languages, which helps the model to understand and generate text in various languages. We evaluated the model on the XGLUE *(Liang et al., 2020)* and M3KE *(Lin et al., 2021)* benchmarks, which are designed to evaluate the model’s performance on multi-lingual tasks. The model shows strong performance on these benchmarks, demonstrating its ability to understand and generate text in various languages.

### 3.3 Reasoning Capabilities

We evaluated Mistral Large 2 on several reasoning benchmarks, including the MMLU *(Hendrycks et al., 2021)*, GSM8K *(Cobbe et al., 2021)*, and BBH *(Suzgun et al., 2022)* benchmarks. The model shows strong performance on these benchmarks, demonstrating its ability to perform complex reasoning tasks. The model’s performance on these benchmarks is particularly impressive, as it shows that the model can understand and reason about a wide range of tasks and domains.

| Model | MMLU | GSM8K | BBH | CodeContests |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Mistral 7B v0.1 | 55.7 | 42.1 | 53.0 | 35.8 |
| Mistral 7B v0.2 | 59.4 | 49.2 | 57.6 | 39.2 |
| Mistral 7B v0.3 | 60.7 | 50.6 | 59.3 | 41.1 |

Table 1: Evaluation of Mistral Large 2 on various benchmarks.

## 4 Conclusion

In this paper, we presented Mistral Large 2, a family of large language models with 12.9 billion and 7 billion parameters. The model demonstrates strong performance on a variety of benchmarks, including code generation, multi-lingual tasks, and reasoning tasks. We also released Mistral 7B v0.3, an optimized version of our 7B model with improved performance on common benchmarks. We believe that Mistral Large 2 represents a significant step forward in the development of large language models, and we hope that it will be a useful tool for a wide range of applications.

What else can I do to resolve this issue?

Last edited: Aug 20, 2018

The error means that Python needs the Visual C++ Build Tools to compile some of the code it needs for SciPy. This is a common error when installing packages which require compilation. Try this:

- Open a command prompt as administrator (type cmd in the start menu, right click on the command prompt and select "Run as administrator")
- Run the following commands one at a time:

```
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
python -m pip install --upgrade setuptools
python -m pip install --upgrade wheel
python -m pip install scipy
```

This should work. If it doesn't, you may need to install the Microsoft Visual C++ Build Tools manually. To do that, follow these steps:

- Download the Visual Studio 2017 Build Tools installer from the Microsoft website. You can get it from this link: https://www.microsoft.com/en-ca/download/details.aspx?id=8433
- Run the installer and install the "C++ Build Tools" workload. You can do this by selecting "C++ Build Tools" on the workloads tab.
- Open a command prompt as administrator and run the commands listed above.

Let me know if that works or if you run into any other issues.

# Mistral V7: Open and Efficient Large Language Model

###### Abstract

We present the Mistral V7 model, a new open large language model built for efficiency. The model is available in 3 sizes: 7B, 12B, and 24B parameters. It is trained on over 6 trillion tokens, using a mixture of open and proprietary data, and is optimized to be deployed efficiently. We show that the model performs on par with leading closed-source models of similar size and outperforms them in many code-related tasks. The model is also designed to be easily fine-tuned and we show that a version fine-tuned on a small dataset of 100K examples can perform on par with a model trained on billions of examples. The model is available under the Apache 2.0 license, and we provide a fully open-source implementation.

## 1 Introduction

In 2023, the open-source community saw a surge of interest in large language models (LLMs), with many models being released at a rapid pace. Despite the impressive capabilities of these models, there was still a significant gap in performance between the leading open-source models and the closed-source models. In particular, the open-source models were often not as efficient as their closed-source counterparts, requiring more computational resources to train and deploy. In this work, we introduce Mistral V7, a new open large language model that bridges this gap. Our primary focus is on efficiency: we aim to provide a model that can be trained and deployed with minimal computational resources, while still achieving state-of-the-art performance. To this end, we make several key contributions:

- We present a new model architecture that is optimized for efficiency. The model is designed to be trained and deployed with minimal computational resources, while still achieving state-of-the-art performance.
- We use a new data mixture that combines open and proprietary data. The data is carefully curated to ensure that the model is both powerful and safe.
- We show that the model performs on par with leading closed-source models of similar size and outperforms them in many code-related tasks.
- We design the model to be easily fine-tuned. We show that a version fine-tuned on a small dataset of 100K examples can perform on par with a model trained on billions of examples.

The Mistral V7 model is available in three sizes: 7B, 12B, and 24B parameters. The models are available under the Apache 2.0 license, and we provide a fully open-source implementation. We hope that this model will be useful for the open-source community and will help to bridge the gap between open-source and closed-source models.

## 2 Model Architecture

The Mistral V7 model is based on the transformer architecture *(Vaswani et al., 2017)*, with several key modifications that are designed to improve efficiency and performance. The model uses a mixture of experts (MoE) architecture *(Shazeer et al., 2017)*, where each layer has a set of experts that are specialized for different types of data. The model is trained using a new data mixture that combines open and proprietary data, which is carefully curated to ensure that the model is both powerful and safe. The model is also designed to be easily fine-tuned, with a simple and efficient fine-tuning procedure that can be used to adapt the model to new tasks.

### 2.1 Model Design

The Mistral V7 model is a transformer-based architecture with several key modifications that are designed to improve efficiency and performance. The model uses a mixture of experts (MoE) architecture *(Shazeer et al., 2017)*, where each layer has a set of experts that are specialized for different types of data. This allows the model to focus its computational resources on the most relevant parts of the input, which can improve both efficiency and performance. The model also uses a new attention mechanism that is designed to be more efficient than the standard self-attention mechanism. This mechanism reduces the computational complexity of the model, which can improve both training and inference speed.
